latest.title: Result of AGM
latest.content:
11 Dec 2025 17:15
RNS Number : 2737L
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
11 December 2025
ALTERNATIVE LIQUIDITY FUND LIMITED
Registered in Guernsey - Number 60552
LEI NUMBER 213800R5CHD76J3LU713
Registered Office:
1 Royal Plaza, Royal Avenue,
St Peter Port, Guernsey, GY1 2HL
11 December 2025
ANNUAL GENERAL MEETING VOTING RESULTS
The Board of Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited (the "
Company
") is pleased to announce the results of the Annual General Meeting ("
AGM
") which was held at the registered office of the Company at 11.00am on 11 December 2025. Resolutions 1 to 4 were proposed as Ordinary Resolutions and Resolution 5 was proposed as a Special Resolution.
The result of the voting was:
Resolution 1: THAT the Directors be and are hereby authorised to re-appoint Grant Thornton as the Company's auditor, and to fix the auditors' remuneration
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 2: THAT Mr Quentin Spicer be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,158,362 (92.33%)
Votes against: 7,069,259
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 3: THAT Dr Richard Berman be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 4: THAT Mr Anthony Pickford be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 5: THAT the Company is approved to continue in existence and the continued implementation by the Board of the Investment Policy and Strategy be approved.
Votes for: 92,227,621 (100.00%)
Votes against: NIL
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed

As at the close of business on 30 June 2025, the final Net Asset Value per share of the Company is as follows:
Fund Name
NAV
Quarterly Performance
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
USD 0.1569
151%
As previously announced, realisation of assets within the Company's portfolio since the end of June, together with the announcement of a distribution to shareholders of $18.99mln being equivalent to $0.13 (13 cents) a share, result in the June 30 NAV being significantly different to the current position. Therefore, in addition to publishing the June 30 NAV, the Board has taken the decision to provide an indicative NAV as at September 30 that incorporates changes to the underlying portlio over the past quarter.
The Board estimates that as at the close of business on 30 September 2025, the indicative Net Asset Value per share of the Company is as follows:
Fund Name
NAV
Quarterly Performance
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
USD 0.0365
154% - see Note
Note: the indicative quarterly performance for Sep 30 comprises the distribution to shareholders plus a slight uplift in the underlying portfolio value.
An analysis of the portfolio as at June 30 and September 30 2025 is provided in the quarterly factsheet which is now available on the Company's website, please see the link below.
https://alternativeliquidityfund.com/fact-sheets-and-fund-updates/2025/
Please note that the fact sheet will contain the detailed portfolio update, the Board's observations and conclusions as indicated in the previous shareholder notification of September 22nd.
The final September NAV will be published in due course.

company_30s.what_they_do: Allanfield Group is a small UK company that invests in and manages real‑estate assets, primarily residential and commercial properties.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The real‑estate sector in the UK continues to generate steady demand for both rental and development projects. Allanfield’s focus on niche property markets gives it a modest competitive edge in a crowded space.
company_30s.current_state: The company has been trading on AIM for several years but has limited public financial information. Recent filings show routine shareholder updates and modest portfolio changes, suggesting the business is operating at a very small scale.

rns_narrative: The latest RNSs revolve around shareholder governance and capital management. An AGM was held, followed by a notice of receipt of funds and a return of capital to investors. The company also disclosed recent holdings and a portfolio update, indicating that management is keeping the book tidy.
These events point to a company that is primarily focused on maintaining shareholder relations and managing a small, static asset base rather than pursuing aggressive growth.

There is no evidence of a new development or large investment that would change the company’s trajectory, but the consistent flow of capital suggests that the management team is keeping the business afloat.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silently watching – investors are on the sidelines, waiting for a clear signal.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: No active bullish chatter in recent posts; the only bullish comments are from 2011–2016 and do not reflect current sentiment.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: No specific bearish thesis is circulating; the lack of discussion itself signals indifference.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The community remains quiet. There has been no change in tone over the past year, and the silence is consistent with a low‑profile, low‑volume stock.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for Allanfield Group is flat. No recent spikes in search volume, which means any news or price movement could catch the market off‑guard.

near_term_catalysts: An upcoming AGM or shareholder meeting could reveal a new capital‑raising plan or a change in the company’s investment strategy.
If the company announces the acquisition of a new property or a partnership that expands its portfolio, the price could rally.
A sudden uptick in trading volume that exceeds 200% of the 20‑day average would signal renewed interest and could trigger a bounce.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals to monitor over the next two to three months are: (1) a volume spike at or near the current support level, (2) a new RNS that announces a capital infusion or a significant asset purchase, and (3) any change in the company’s holdings that suggests institutional interest.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stage of a potential recovery. The APEX compression score is high, indicating a tight price range that could be primed for a breakout once a catalyst emerges.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: Allanfield has been trading at a very low price for an extended period, and the market has largely ignored it. If the company can secure a new investment or announce a strategic property acquisition, the price could climb to the mid‑range of its historical 52‑week range. A 50% rebound from the current level would represent a sizeable upside, especially given the low entry cost.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risk is that the company may remain stagnant, with no new assets or capital, and the price could continue to drift lower. A further decline to 20% of the current level would wipe out most of the invested capital.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is capped at the small amount invested, while upside could be 4‑to‑1 if the price recovers to the middle of the 52‑week range.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price breaks above the current resistance level (around 0.02p) on a volume spike of at least 150% of the 20‑day average. Size the position so that the stop‑loss is 10% below the entry point. Use a trailing stop to lock in gains once the price moves higher.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until a clear catalyst appears – a new RNS announcing a capital raise, a property acquisition, or a volume surge. If any of these happen, consider a small entry at the new support level.
playbook.if_passing: If the company is delisted or files for bankruptcy, exit immediately. Otherwise, no immediate red flag warrants abandoning the contrarian thesis.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Allanfield Group sits on a deep, silent crash; a forthcoming capital or asset announcement could trigger a surprise rebound, offering a high‑asymmetry upside for those willing to wait for the right catalyst.
